hero.headline: Uttaranchal University Online MBA in HR Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved online MBA with HR Management specialisation from Uttaranchal University, Dehradun. NAAC A+, WES recognised, est. 2011. Rs 1,40,000 gross, Rs 98,000 net (semester) or Rs 94,000 net (one-time) after 30% Early Bird. No-cost EMI at Rs 3,956/month. Training and Development, Performance and Compensation Management, Industrial Relations, and HRM Planning in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: Uttaranchal University Online MBA HR Management: NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, WES recognised. Rs 94,000 net (one-time) or Rs 98,000 net (semester) after 30% Early Bird on Rs 1,40,000 gross. No-cost EMI at Rs 3,956/month. Sem 3-4 depth in Training and Development Practices, Performance and Compensation Management, Industrial Relations and Labor Laws, and HRM Planning Bargaining and Negotiation Process. Budget-friendly HR MBA with solid labour law coverage.

about.body:
The Uttaranchal University Online MBA in HR Management is offered by Uttaranchal University (UU), a private university established in 2011 in Dehradun, Uttarakhand. UU holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes. The degree is WES recognised.

The HR Management specialisation covers Training and Development Practices, Performance and Compensation Management, Industrial Relations and Labor Laws, and HRM Planning Bargaining and Negotiation Process across Semesters 3 and 4. The inclusion of Industrial Relations and Labor Laws is a differentiator. Many online MBA HR programmes skip IR and labour law depth, which limits graduates in manufacturing, unionised, and compliance-heavy environments.

At Rs 94,000 net (one-time), this is one of the most affordable NAAC A+ online MBAs with a dedicated HR track. The trade-off is limited brand recognition outside Uttarakhand and North India. For HR professionals who need the credential and labour law knowledge at minimum cost, UU HR Management is a practical choice.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Develop competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis models, and corporate-level strategic planning skills.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Study procurement, logistics, inventory management, and end-to-end supply chain coordination.

sem3Subjects.name: Financial Statement Analysis
sem3Subjects.description: Interpret balance sheets, income statements, cash flow statements, and apply ratio analysis for decision-making.

sem3Subjects.name: Training and Development Practices
sem3Subjects.description: Design training needs analysis, learning programme delivery, training ROI measurement, and employee development frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Performance and Compensation Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build performance appraisal systems, design compensation structures, incentive plans, and pay equity frameworks.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
sem4Subjects.description: Study business model creation, startup finance, venture capital, and entrepreneurial risk assessment.

sem4Subjects.name: Corporate Governance
sem4Subjects.description: Examine board structures, regulatory compliance, SEBI guidelines, and ethical governance frameworks.

sem4Subjects.name: Project Work
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied research project on an HR management topic with data collection, analysis, and presentation.

sem4Subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labor Laws
sem4Subjects.description: Study the Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, Trade Unions Act, collective bargaining, and workplace dispute resolution.

sem4Subjects.name: HRM Planning Bargaining and Negotiation Process
sem4Subjects.description: Apply workforce planning models, manpower forecasting, negotiation tactics, and collective bargaining process management.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 cover shared core subjects across all Uttaranchal University Online MBA specialisations. See the main Uttaranchal University Online MBA page for the core subject list.

whoHires.body:
HR Management MBA graduates target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT and IT services, Manufacturing, BFSI, Consulting firms, FMCG and retail, Healthcare and pharma

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Associate, Compensation and Benefits Analyst, Training Coordinator, HR Operations Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Learning and Development Manager, Compensation Manager, HR Business Partner

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO, VP Human Resources, Head of Talent Acquisition, Director L&D, Head of HR Operations

**Typical hiring organisations:** TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL Technologies, Cognizant, Accenture, Deloitte, KPMG, Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, Mahindra, Reliance Industries, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Hindustan Unilever

skills.body:
The HR Management specialisation builds skills tested by HR departments across industries:

**Technical skills:**
- Training and Development: training needs analysis, learning programme design, delivery methods, and training ROI measurement for L&D roles
- Performance and Compensation Management: KPI-based appraisal systems, compensation benchmarking, pay equity analysis, and variable pay design for C&B roles
- Industrial Relations and Labor Laws: Industrial Disputes Act application, Trade Unions Act compliance, collective bargaining, and workplace dispute resolution for IR and compliance roles
- HRM Planning: workforce forecasting, succession planning, manpower budgeting, and HR analytics for strategic HR roles

**Applied skills:**
- Negotiation: collective bargaining preparation, union negotiation tactics, and stakeholder management during disputes
- HR Strategy: linking HR planning to business strategy, building talent pipelines, and designing retention frameworks

**Soft skills:** Employee grievance handling, cross-functional collaboration with operations and finance, presenting HR metrics to leadership, and managing change communication during restructuring.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Uttaranchal University Online MBA HR Management against peers:

**vs IGNOU MBA HR (Rs 66,000):** IGNOU is the cheapest option with government university brand. IGNOU covers HR broadly but lacks a modern LMS. UU at Rs 94,000 offers dedicated Industrial Relations and Labor Laws plus structured online delivery. For labour law depth at affordable cost, UU has an edge.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA HR (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is NAAC A+ at a similar budget tier with a mandatory Master Thesis. Both are North India focused. UU offers dedicated IR and Labor Laws as a separate subject, which Galgotias may not. Fee difference is under Rs 8,000.

**vs Amity Online MBA HR (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is 2.4x the cost with NIRF #22 and larger alumni network. For pan-India HR roles at mid-to-large corporates, Amity HR carries stronger recognition. UU delivers NAAC A+ at under Rs 1 lakh.

**vs LPU Online MBA HR (Rs 1,61,000):** LPU offers NIRF #31, Coursera Plus, and stronger placement infrastructure. At 1.7x the cost, LPU is a meaningfully stronger package for HR careers.

**vs CU Online MBA HR (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NIRF #19 with PwC co-teaching. At 1.75x the cost, CU offers substantially better rankings and industry integration.

**vs JIIT Online MBA (Rs 1,82,000):** JIIT offers mandatory internship and AI core. At nearly 2x the cost, better for tech-adjacent HR roles.

**vs DPU Online MBA HR (Rs 1,89,400):** DPU is NIRF #41, NAAC A++. At 2x the cost, DPU offers higher accreditation grade and Maharashtra regional strength.

fees.body:
The Uttaranchal University Online MBA in HR Management has a gross fee of Rs 1,40,000. A 30% Early Bird discount reduces the net fee across all payment tracks.

**Track 1: One-Time Payment**
- Net fee: Rs 94,000 (includes Rs 4,000 additional concession over semester track)
- Single payment at admission

**Track 2: Annual Payment**
- Rs 47,000 x 2 years = Rs 94,000 net
- Same net cost as one-time payment

**Track 3: Semester Payment**
- Rs 24,500 x 4 semesters = Rs 98,000 net
- Costs Rs 4,000 more than one-time or annual tracks

**No-Cost EMI:**
- Rs 3,956/month directly through the university
- No interest charged. No processing fee.

**Included:** Exam fee of Rs 2,500 per semester is included in all quoted figures.
**Not applicable:** No separate application fee. No scholarships beyond the Early Bird discount. No bundled perks.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Uttaranchal University each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Uttaranchal University, Dehradun. It reads 'Master of Business Administration' with the HR Management specialisation noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The degree is WES recognised for international credential evaluation. It is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Uttaranchal University Online MBA HR Management students. Ratings from post-completion feedback.

items.name: Meera
items.liked: Industrial Relations and Labor Laws was the strongest subject. Directly applicable to my role at a manufacturing unit. The Rs 94,000 fee with NAAC A+ is outstanding value.
items.disliked: No SHRM or HRCI certification alignment. If you need global HR certifications, you will have to pursue them separately.
items.body: I work in HR at a pharma manufacturing plant in Haridwar. The IR and labour law subjects were exactly what I needed for compliance and union management. UU brand is well-known in Uttarakhand industry circles. For manufacturing HR roles in the region, this MBA delivered what I needed at a price that made sense.

items.name: Arjun
items.liked: Performance and Compensation Management gave me frameworks I use daily in my C&B role. No-cost EMI was a genuine help.
items.disliked: Brand recognition is weak in Gurgaon's IT corridor. HR managers at large IT companies did not recognise UU. I had to explain the NAAC A+ tag.
items.body: If you work in North India's manufacturing or SME sector, UU HR is a solid credential. If you are targeting HR roles at IT majors or MNCs in metro cities, invest in LPU, Amity, or CU. The brand gap is real, even with NAAC A+. Good programme for the fee, but manage your brand expectations.

items.name: Divya
items.liked: HRM Planning and Negotiation Process was well-taught. The semester fee of Rs 24,500 was genuinely manageable on a junior HR salary.
items.disliked: Career services and placement support for online students are minimal. The university does not actively connect online MBA grads with recruiters.
items.body: This MBA is for people who already have HR jobs and need the qualification. It is not a career-launch programme. I used it to move from HR executive to HR manager at my existing company. For that purpose, UU HR Management at Rs 94,000 was the right investment.

faqs.question: Does UU Online MBA HR Management cover labour laws?
faqs.answer: Yes. Industrial Relations and Labor Laws is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering the Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, Trade Unions Act, collective bargaining, and workplace dispute resolution.

faqs.question: What is the net fee for UU Online MBA HR Management?
faqs.answer: The gross fee is Rs 1,40,000. After the 30% Early Bird discount, the net fee is Rs 94,000 (one-time or annual) or Rs 98,000 (semester track). The semester track costs Rs 4,000 more.

faqs.question: Is this MBA aligned with SHRM or HRCI certifications?
faqs.answer: No. UU does not align its HR curriculum with SHRM or HRCI certification frameworks. If you need global HR certification alignment, you will need to pursue SHRM-CP or PHR separately.

faqs.question: Is the degree valid for government jobs?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme is UGC-DEB approved. The degree is valid for government job applications, PSU recruitment, and higher education admission across India.

faqs.question: Is UU HR MBA suitable for manufacturing sector HR roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The dedicated Industrial Relations and Labor Laws subject, combined with HRM Planning and Negotiation Process, makes this programme well-suited for HR professionals in manufacturing, unionised workplaces, and compliance-heavy industries.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved online MBA in HR Management at under Rs 1 lakh total fee.
You need dedicated Industrial Relations and Labor Laws coverage for manufacturing, compliance, or unionised workplace roles.
You want no-cost EMI at Rs 3,956/month with exam fees included and no hidden charges.
consider: If pan-India HR brand recognition matters, Amity (Rs 2.25L, NIRF #22) or LPU (Rs 1.61L, NIRF #31) carry stronger weight in IT and MNC hiring.
If you want SHRM or HRCI certification alignment, those must be pursued separately regardless of which MBA you choose.
If you want industry co-teaching and better rankings, CU at Rs 1.65L (NIRF #19, PwC modules) is a stronger package.
